data.title: Etsy falls on holiday-quarter sales miss
data.description:
** Shares of online marketplace Etsy down 7% at $52.76 premarket

** Co misses Q4 estimates for revenue and gross merchandise sales on softer sales on its two-way marketplace

** Gross merchandise sales (GMS) fall 6.8% year-over-year to $3.74 billion, compared with estimates of a 3.2% fall to $3.88 billion -LSEG

** Q4 revenue of $852.2 mln misses analysts' estimates of $862.8 mln

data.title: Virturo Enhances Trading Efficiency with Advanced Automation and Risk Strategies
data.description:
LONDON, Feb. 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Virturo, a leader in CFD trading and financial technology, has launched a suite of AI-driven automated trading and advanced risk management solutions, designed to enhance investment strategies for high-net-worth traders. By integrating cutting-edge technology with expert insights, Virturo enables traders to optimize performance while effectively managing market volatility.

The Power of Automation in Modern Trading

“Automated trading features greatly enhance a trader’s ability to capitalize on market opportunities without the emotional stress of manual trading,” says Michael Stean, Senior Financial Strategist at Virturo. “At Virturo, we provide traders with the tools to optimize performance and maximize efficiency.”

Automated trading has transformed financial markets, enabling traders to execute strategies efficiently without constant market monitoring. With Virturo’s automation tools, traders can:

  • Set predefined entry and exit points to execute trades with precision.
  • Eliminate emotional decision-making, ensuring disciplined execution.
  • Respond instantly to market changes, capitalizing on opportunities in real-time.

Risk Management Meets Cutting-Edge Technology

Smart trading isn’t just about speed - it’s about control. Virturo’s advanced risk management features work alongside automation to protect investments and maximize returns, including:

  • Limit Orders - Executing trades only at the desired price point to control entry and exit precision.
  • Take-Profit & Stop-Loss Orders - Locking in gains and minimize losses with predefined price thresholds.
  • Conditional Orders - Automating trade actions based on specific market conditions, removing uncertainty from execution.

“When risk management is integrated with automation, it not only protects investments but also enhances a trader’s potential for success,” adds Stean.

Tailored for High-Value Investors

Virturo’s sophisticated trading ecosystem is designed for high-net-worth traders who require precision, speed, and strategic execution. The platform’s advanced features include:

  • Dynamic margin optimization to enhance capital efficiency.
  • Trading pyramiding strategies to scale profitable positions intelligently.
  • Portfolio hedging tools to safeguard against market volatility.

“At Virturo, we provide high-net-worth traders with a tailored blend of technology and expertise,” explains Stean. “Automation amplifies the efficiency of managing complex portfolios while ensuring every decision aligns with long-term financial goals.”

Virturo’s Commitment to Smart, Data-Driven Trading

While automation enhances execution speed, Virturo ensures traders retain full strategic control. The platform integrates AI-driven analysis with expert guidance, allowing traders to fine-tune strategies, adapt to evolving markets, and make informed decisions with confidence.

“The future of trading belongs to those who embrace automation, predictive analytics, and risk-focused strategies,” says Stean. “Virturo’s innovative platform delivers the tools to navigate market complexities while optimizing performance.”

Virturo continues to lead the next generation of trading, offering elite investors the power of AI, automation, and expert-backed risk management in one seamless platform.

Users can discover the next evolution of trading at www.virturo.com.

About Virturo

Virturo, a leading broker in CFD trading and financial technology, is redefining investment strategies with its AI-driven automated trading and advanced risk management solutions.

Website LinkedIn Twitter YouTube Facebook

Contact

Media Team

Virturo

support@virturo.com

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/5b4f70a2-1892-46ae-bbf0-b3e7527cb899

data.title: Futures dip after Trump ratchets up tariff threats; Fed minutes on tap
data.description:
Key points:
  • Wix.com rises after fourth-quarter profit beats estimates
  • Payments processor Shift4 to buy Global Blue in $2.5-bln deal
  • Futures down: Dow 0.25%, S&P 500 0.17%, Nasdaq 0.15%

U.S. stock index futures slipped on Wednesday as investors examined President Donald Trump's latest pronouncements on trade restrictions and awaited minutes of the Federal Reserve's January meeting, expected to be released later in the day.

The U.S. president said on Tuesday that he intends to impose auto tariffs "in the neighborhood of 25%", along with similar duties on semiconductor and pharmaceuticals imports.

Minutes of the U.S. central bank's January policy meeting - where voting members elected to let interest rates stand and Fed Chair Jerome Powell said there would be no rush to cut them again until inflation and jobs data made it appropriate - are scheduled to be released at 2 p.m. ET.

Investors will look for hints on the Fed's rate path in the minutes in light of last week's mixed consumer and producer prices data, coupled with a sharp drop in retail sales, which had sent Treasury yields lower.

"Keep an eye out for further discussions on the impact of Trump's policies and possible insights into how members have changed their views on the timing of further rate cuts, if any, compared to the December forecasts," Karl Steiner, head of analysis at SEB, said in a note.

Traders currently see at least one 25-basis-point rate cut and a 44% chance of an additional lowering by December, according to LSEG data.

Since his inauguration in January, Trump has imposed a 10% tariff on all imports from China, on top of existing levies. He also announced and delayed for a month 25% tariffs on goods from Mexico and non-energy imports from Canada.

The S&P 500 eked out an all-time closing high in the last session, lifting tech heavyweights Microsoft and Nvidia .

At 06:47 a.m. ET, Dow E-minis were down 113 points, or 0.25%, S&P 500 E-minis were down 10.75 points, or 0.17%, and Nasdaq 100 E-minis were down 32.5 points, or 0.15%.

Retail giant Walmart's results, a bellwether for how the American consumer is faring, are due later this week.

Arista Networks dropped 4.6% despite the cloud computing provider forecasting first-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates.

Bumble slid 17.8% after the dating app operator forecast first-quarter revenue below analysts' estimates.

Specialty chemicals company Celanese lost 13.1% after reporting a quarterly loss.

Payments processor Shift4 fell 9.9% after its fourth-quarter results and news that the company has agreed to buy Global Blue in a deal valued at $2.5 billion. Shares of Global Blue rose 19.1%.

Website builder Wix.com advanced 5.9% after beating fourth-quarter net profit expectations.

Ruisen Life Service Appoints Former Roiserv Lifestyle Services Exec as President
data.description:
Ruisen Life Service appointed Liu Yonggang as its president effective from Friday, Feb. 21 whereas Xie Chenguang will cease to be the acting president from the same date, a Wednesday bourse filing said.

Liu Yonggang has over 20 years of professional experience in corporate strategy and operational management in the property management and real estate industries and has previously served as an executive director and general manager of Roiserv Lifestyle Services .

data.title: Saudi Aramco to Buy 25% Stake in Unioil Petroleum Philippines
data.description:
Saudi Arabian Oil Co. (SASE:2222), d/b/a Saudi Aramco, said Wednesday that it agreed to acquire a 25% stake in Unioil Petroleum Philippines, marking its entry into the Philippines retail market.

The oil giant said the deal, which is subject to customary closing conditions, is part of its global retail network expansion strategy. Unioil Petroleum is involved in the retail, wholesale, and storage of downstream fuels with 165 retail locations in the Philippines.

data.title: Wolverine World Wide Swings to 4Q Profit Despite Revenue Decline
data.description:
By Denny Jacob

Wolverine World Wide posted a profit in its fourth quarter despite declining revenue.

The footwear maker known for brands including Saucony and Hush Puppies logged earnings of $24.6 million, or 29 cents a share, compared to a loss of $91.2 million, or $1.15 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, earnings came in at 48 cents a share. Analysts polled by FactSet expected 42 cents a share.

Revenue declined to $494.7 million from $526.7 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $485.7 million.

"In the fourth quarter, we exceeded our expectations for revenue and earnings and inflected to growth as a company," said Chief Executive Chris Hufnagel. He added that the company's brands are poised to continue building momentum in 2025 after an earlier enacted turnaround strategy bore fruit.

The Rockford, Mich., company forecast revenue to be between $1.80 billion and $1.83 billion with adjusted earnings per-share in the range of $1.05 to $1.20 for fiscal 2025. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.86 billion in revenue as well as $1.34 a share in adjusted earnings.

Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com

data.title: Global Energy Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Energy markets. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1220 GMT - European natural-gas prices continue to trade below the 50-euros-a-megawatt-hour mark following reports that the EU will work on more flexible storage refilling targets. The European Commission is set to work with member states to promote more flexible storage requirements due to concerns over strict deadlines and high prices, Reuters says, citing a draft EU document due to be published next week. EU countries are currently required to refill their caverns to 90% of capacity by November. As of Monday, gas inventories were 43.4% full, well below last year's levels due to colder temperatures and low wind speeds. The benchmark Dutch TTF contract currently trades 0.8% to 49.64 euros a megawatt hour, but is down 11% on the week. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

1128 GMT - SGL Carbon's restructuring of its Carbon Fibers unit doesn't surprise as a sale of the entire business was no longer realistic, Stifel analysts say in a note. The German carbon manufacturing company said it would restructure the unit for 50 million euros over two years and ruled out a sale of the business. Its free cash flow might not be positive anymore in 2025 due to the restructuring costs, they say. Shares fall 2.4% to 4.08 euros. (helena.smolak@wsj.com)

1053 GMT - Orsted's earnings guidance looks reasonable, but the company still faces several overhangs, Jefferies analysts write. They see a risk of further impairments after Orsted flagged around 5 billion Danish kroner of impairments if the 10% bonus investment tax credit doesn't come through for its Sunrise and Revolution U.S. wind farms. "Further, we note the risk of further delays/complications during construction, given Orsted's recent track record." Jefferies says it's possible Orsted can achieve its target for disposal proceeds, but it's unclear if it can sell key assets at the required valuations under current challenging market conditions. Jefferies lowers its price target to 340 kroner from 370 kroner and keeps at hold. Shares rise 1.8% higher to 304 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0933 GMT - The potential easing of Western sanctions on Russia is unlikely to significantly increase Moscow's oil flows, according to Goldman Sachs. Traders are closely monitoring talks between the U.S. and Russia aimed at ending the war in Ukraine, as a peace deal could lead to the lifting of sanctions and resumption of some Russian exports to Europe. However, "Russia crude oil production is constrained by its OPEC+ 9 million barrels a day production target rather than current sanctions, which are affecting the destination but not the volume of oil exports," analysts at the U.S. bank say. Goldman also says it expects OPEC and its allies to delay plans to start raising output in April, citing higher compliance with quotas from Russia and other producers as well as the strategic advantage of keeping options open amid high uncertainty surrounding U.S. policy.(giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0908 GMT - Oil prices rise in early trading amid market concerns over supply disruptions. Brent crude is up 0.8% at $76.46 a barrel, while WTI trades 0.9% higher to $72.45 a barrel. Gains are largely driven by fears over Kazakhstan's oil flows following a drone attack against a major pumping station in southern Russia. Meanwhile, the Group of Seven is reportedly considering tightening the current oil price cap of $60/bbl on Russia in an effort to cut its revenues. Traders are also waiting to see whether OPEC and its allies will stick to their current output plans or delay them once again. "A delay could wipe out the surplus we expect for the market this year, which would leave prices better supported," ING analysts say. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0730 GMT - Comex gold futures' bullish momentum has reaccelerated, based on the daily chart, RHB Retail Research's Joseph Chai says in a commentary. Tuesday's price movements indicate the precious metal has resumed its bullish trajectory after undergoing profit-taking last Friday, the analyst notes. The relative strength index is rounding upward, signaling that bullish momentum is picking up speed, Chai says. The commodity is trading above both 20- and 50-day simple moving averages, which maintains the bullish technical setup. The nearest resistance is pegged at $3,000/oz, the analyst adds. Spot gold is little changed at $2,934.78/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

SolarEdge Technologies Reports Q4 and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
SolarEdge Technologies, Inc., a global leader in smart energy technology, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company, known for its innovative solar energy solutions, has faced a challenging year but is optimistic about future growth and profitability.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, SolarEdge reported revenues of $196.2 million, a decrease of 17% from the previous quarter's $235.4 million. The solar segment contributed $189.0 million to the total revenue, down 15% from $222.1 million in the prior quarter. The company experienced a significant write-down and impairment of assets amounting to $138 million, impacting both GAAP and Non-GAAP financials.

GAAP gross margin for Q4 was negative 57.2%, an improvement from negative 309.1% in the previous quarter. Non-GAAP gross margin also improved to negative 39.5% from negative 305.0%. The company reported a GAAP net loss of $287.4 million, or $5.00 per share, compared to a net loss of $1.231 billion, or $21.58 per share, in the prior quarter. Non-GAAP net loss was $202.5 million, or $3.52 per share, compared to $899.8 million, or $15.78 per share, in the previous quarter.

For the full year 2024, SolarEdge's total revenues were $901.5 million, a significant drop from $2.98 billion in 2023. The solar segment generated $842.4 million in revenue, down from $2.82 billion in the prior year. The company reported a GAAP net loss of $1.81 billion, or $31.64 per share, compared to a net income of $34.3 million, or $0.61 per share, in 2023. Non-GAAP net loss for the year was $1.31 billion, or $22.99 per share, compared to a net income of $248.4 million, or $4.39 per share, in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

During the fourth quarter, SolarEdge shipped 895 MW (AC) of inverters and 130 MWh of batteries for PV applications. For the full year, the company shipped 3,563 MW (AC) of inverters and 576 MWh of batteries. The company also undertook an asset valuation analysis, resulting in a write-down and impairment of various assets totaling $1.17 billion for the year.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

SolarEdge has announced the closure of its Energy Storage business in Korea, which will impact future financial reporting as the company will no longer report segments. The company also revised its previously reported revenues and loans receivables for the third quarter of 2024 due to an amended agreement with a customer.

Management's Perspective

Shuki Nir, CEO of SolarEdge, expressed optimism about the company's future, stating, "There are exciting opportunities ahead for SolarEdge. We are just getting started on our turnaround story. The return to positive free cash flow generation in Q4 is a solid first step, and we expect to be free cash flow positive in Q1 2025 and for the full year 2025."

Future Outlook

For the first quarter of 2025, SolarEdge expects revenues to be within the range of $195 million to $215 million. The company also anticipates a Non-GAAP gross margin of 6% to 10% and Non-GAAP operating expenses between $98 million and $103 million.

SEC Filing: SOLAREDGE TECHNOLOGIES, INC. [ SEDG ] - 8-K - Feb. 19, 2025

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 7 AM ET
data.description:
Dow, Nasdaq Futures Edge Higher

Stock futures made small gains amid President Trump's tariff threats.

Fed Minutes Should Give More Clues About Rate Cut Timing

Minutes from the Federal Open Market Committee's Jan. 28-29 meeting will be released Wednesday afternoon at 2 p.m. Eastern time.

Trump Is Scrambling Global Automakers' Reliance on America

Facing intense competition in China and heavy regulation in Europe, overseas automakers were counting on the U.S. to keep their engines humming. Then came Trump's tariff threats.

U.K. Inflation Reaches 10-Month High

Consumer prices were 3% higher in January than a year earlier, up from 2.5% in December, complicating the picture for the BOE after it said it would cut interest rates gradually.

China Wants Its Economy to Grow 5%. Consumers Aren't Buying It.

Chinese consumers are nervous about the political and economic environments. Their pocketbooks are shut.

Indonesia Central Bank Holds Rates as It Eyes Rupiah, Trade Risks

Bank Indonesia hit the pause button again, holding rates steady after a surprise cut last month as it monitors currency headwinds and rising external risks.

Trump Floats 25% Tariffs on Autos, Chips, Pharmaceuticals

The president provided further details about his expected tariff moves.

China Home Prices Show Slight Improvement But Remain in Decline

Home prices in major Chinese cities continued to slightly narrow their declines, suggesting that Beijing's policy blitz has helped stabilize the beleaguered real-estate sector.

BOJ's Takata Calls for Gradual Rate Hikes, Sees Upside Risks to Prices

The Bank of Japan needs to keep adjusting the degree of monetary accommodation in stages, policy board member Hajime Takata said as he warned about inflation risks.

RBNZ Slashes Rates Further, Sounds Global Trade Warning

The Reserve Bank of New Zealand slashed interest rates by a further 50 basis points on Wednesday, continuing its campaign to restart a stalled economy.

data.title: HSBC Dials Back Climate Ambitions, Following U.S. Banks — WSJ
data.description:
By Joe Wallace

Another major bank is walking back climate commitments made when ESG was all the rage.

HSBC on Wednesday delayed by two decades a goal announced during the pandemic to eliminate greenhouse-gas emissions it is responsible for by 2030, setting a new target of 2050. Chief Executive Georges Elhedery blamed companies in HSBC's supply chain, saying the bank was well on track to reach net-zero emissions at its operations but that suppliers "have been more challenged".

Given that, HSBC would have had to rely heavily on carbon credits to be able to declare itself net-zero by the original target date, the bank said.

A more significant goal the bank laid out in 2020 was not to finance any greenhouse-gas emissions by 2050. That's the date by which the United Nations says the world needs to reach net-zero to limit global warming to less than 1.5 degrees Celsius.

HSBC said Wednesday it was reviewing its interim 2030 targets for financed emissions because the world was taking longer than it anticipated to move toward cleaner sources of energy. "Our influence on the decarbonisation of individual companies and the industries and economies in which our customers operate has limits," it added.

HSBC is still part of a United Nations-backed coalition known as the Net-Zero Banking Alliance. JPMorgan, Bank of America, Citigroup and several other big U.S. banks quit in recent months, reflecting a broad pullback by companies from environmental, social and governance-related initiatives.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Grammer: Their Successors Will Be Confirmed at the Next AGM on May 22
Enpro Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
Enpro Inc., a leading industrial technology company, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company has also introduced its guidance for 2025, highlighting its strategic initiatives and financial performance.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Enpro reported sales of $258.4 million, marking a 3.7% increase compared to the same period last year. The company's GAAP net income from continuing operations improved significantly to $13.9 million, up from a loss of $4.9 million in the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA saw a notable increase of 24.1%, reaching $58.2 million. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations also improved to $0.66, compared to a diluted loss per share of $0.23 last year.

For the full year 2024, sales were slightly down by 1.0% to $1.05 billion. However, GAAP net income from continuing operations increased to $72.9 million, a substantial rise from $10.8 million in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $254.8 million, up 7.1% from the previous year. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations increased to $3.45, compared to $0.51 last year.

Business and Operational Highlights

Enpro's Sealing Technologies segment reported strong performance, with sales increasing by 10.9% in the fourth quarter, driven by demand in aerospace and nuclear markets. The Advanced Surface Technologies segment, however, saw a 6.4% decline in sales due to continued weakness in semiconductor capital equipment spending.

Corporate expenses decreased to $13.4 million in the fourth quarter, down from $14.7 million a year ago, primarily due to a decrease in share-price-based long-term incentive compensation expense.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Enpro has maintained a strong balance sheet and generated $162.9 million of cash flow from continuing operations during 2024. The company ended the year with $236.3 million in cash and $390 million available under its revolving credit facility. Enpro also increased its quarterly dividend by 3.3% to $0.31 per share, marking the tenth consecutive year of dividend increases.

Management's Perspective

Eric Vaillancourt, President and CEO of Enpro, highlighted the company's strong finish to 2024, driven by excellent performance in Sealing Technologies and improvements in the Advanced Surface Technologies segment. Vaillancourt emphasized the resilience of Enpro's businesses despite challenges in semiconductor capital equipment spending and commercial vehicle OEM markets.

"Our strong balance sheet and cash flow generation enable us to pursue high-margin organic growth opportunities and select acquisitions that meet our rigorous strategic and financial criteria," Vaillancourt stated.

Future Outlook

Enpro has introduced its guidance for 2025, expecting revenue growth in the low to mid-single-digit range. The company anticipates adjusted EBITDA to be between $262 million and $277 million, with adjusted diluted earnings per share ranging from $7.00 to $7.70.

SEC Filing: Enpro Inc. [ NPO ] - 8-K - Feb. 19, 2025

data.title: CGC STOCK ALERT: Levi & Korsinsky Notifies Canopy Growth Corporation Investors of an Ongoing Investigation
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 19, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Canopy Growth Corporation ("Canopy Growth") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On February 7, 2025, Canopy issued a press release on the Company's financial results for the third quarter of its fiscal year 2025. Canopy reported year-over-year declines in both net revenue and gross margin, contributing to a wider-than-anticipated net loss for the quarter.

Following this news, Canopy's stock price fell 27.34%. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/canopy-growth-lawsuit-submission-form-2?prid=130464&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

data.title: Super Micro Stock Jumps. How SMCI Rebounded to Become the Top S&P 500 Performer. — Barrons.com
data.description:
George Glover

Super Micro Computer stock was rising yet again on Wednesday, putting the server maker on course to extend its recent winning streak.

Shares, which trade under the ticker SMCI, climbed 4.2% to $58.16 in premarket trading, while futures tracking the S&P 500 were down 0.1%.

Super Micro underlined its zero-to-hero comeback on Tuesday with a 16% gain that took its total gains for 2025 to 83%. That meant it passed software platform provider Palantir Technologies to become the S&P 500's best performer of the year so far.

Investors can thank a stellar recent business update, where Super Micro issued a longer-term revenue outlook that was well above analysts' expectations. That's helped the market look past the company's well-documented accounting issues, which caused the stock to plummet over the final third of 2024, although questions remain: The company has yet to file accounts for the year ended June 30, although Super Micro said in last week's update that it expects to do so before next Tuesday — the deadline the Nasdaq has set to avoid a delisting.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: LC Logistics Anticipates Up to 249% Higher Profit for 2024
data.description:
LC Logistics expects a profit between 359 million yuan and 419 million yuan for 2024, 199% to 249% higher than 120 million yuan logged for 2023, a Wednesday bourse filing said.

The company attributed the expected jump in profit to its business restructuring and asset optimization.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Cuts Curtiss-Wright Price Target to $431 From $452, Maintains Buy Rating
data.description: Curtiss-Wright has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $406.63, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: OneSpaWorld Q4 Adjusted Net Income, Revenue Increase; Q1 Guidance Issued
data.description:
OneSpaWorld reported Q4 adjusted net income Wednesday of $0.20 per diluted share, up from $0.12 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.21.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $217.2 million, up from $194.8 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $213.9 million.

For Q1, the health and beauty services provider expects revenue of $215 million to $220 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $229.2 million.

The company reaffirmed its 2025 revenue outlook of $950 million to $970 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $968.6 million.

Shares of the company were down more than 5% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: Tennis-WADA settlement on three-month ban not unique to Sinner case, says chief counsel
data.description:
Jannik Sinner accepting an immediate three-month ban after reaching a settlement with the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) is not unique to the case of the men's world number one, WADA's chief counsel said.

Sinner was cleared of wrongdoing after testing positive for the anabolic agent clostebol but accepted the ban after WADA was ready to appeal the decision at the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS), where he risked a suspension of up to two years.

The ban will allow Sinner to return before the French Open in May and although he will miss Masters tournaments, the decision has been criticised by current and former players and described as "too convenient".

However, Ross Wenzel, the chief counsel of WADA Tennis, said that although Sinner did no wrong, it was the anti-doping body's duty to appeal the decision as athletes bear responsibility for their negligence by members of their support team.

"We appealed it because of the responsibility that athletes have under our rules, under the code for their entourage... We struck a case resolution agreement, which WADA has entered into 70-odd of these in the last four years," Wenzel told Reuters.

"This is not something that is unique to the case of Mr Sinner. We've done it with athletes at all levels.

"The facts of this case are there for everyone to see and to read. We feel that we've been transparent in the way that we've handled it.

"We feel that given the unique facts of this case ... a three-month period of ineligibility is the right one and a fair one."

NEGATIVE SAMPLES

Wenzel said WADA looked back at all of Sinner's samples for the 12 months prior to the two positive ones in March, and they all came back as negative.

"There was nothing.. it might be a complex factual scenario, but it was well substantiated and the science ruled out any sort of doping scenario," he added.

Former world number one and 24-times Grand Slam champion Novak Djokovic, who co-founded the Professional Tennis Players Association (PTPA), said a majority of players do not feel the process is fair.

Women's world number two Iga Swiatek also escaped a lengthy ban while accepting a one-month suspension last year after testing positive for banned substance trimetazidine (TMZ).

Although Djokovic did not suggest they had intentionally done anything wrong, he said many players feel that favouritism is involved.

"Everyone is entitled to their opinion and some of the people that have expressed their opinions in this case, they're great champions," Wenzel added.

"All I would say is that really read the detailed facts of this case as they're set out in the decision by the Independent Tribunal from August... really engage with those facts and understand."

A tribunal had accepted that clostebol entered Sinner's system from a member of his support team through massages and sports therapy.

"If we think this is a case where Mr Sinner should have been banned for 12 months or 24 months - well, then simply we have a difference of opinion. That was not WADA's view," Wenzel said.
